name: General Principle
summary: A data controller shall not process personal data about a data subject unless the data subject has given his consent to the processing, or the processing is necessary for one or more of the specified lawful purposes. This is the foundational principle that conditions all personal data processing under the PDPA.

obligations.obligation: Obtain valid consent from the data subject before processing personal data
Ensure that where processing relies on a lawful basis other than consent, that basis is clearly established and documented prior to processing
Ensure consent is explicit, informed, and freely given — not bundled with other consents or obtained through deceptive means
obligations.basis: Section 6(1) read with Section 2 (definition of consent)

obligations.obligation: Maintain records of the consent obtained, including the date, method, and scope of consent for each processing activity

Where processing is based on legitimate interests, conduct and document a legitimate interest assessment (LIA) to verify that the interests of the data controller are not overridden by the interests or rights of the data subject

exceptions.exception: Protection of vital interests of data subject
Exercise of any functions conferred by law
Legitimate Interests pursued by the data controller (subject to LIA)
exceptions.conditions: Must conduct LIA; interests must not be overridden by data subject rights.

name: Notice and Choice Principle
summary: A data controller shall inform the data subject of the purposes, classes of third parties, and rights to access/correct data, and whether provision of data is obligatory.

obligations.obligation: Inform whether data provision is obligatory or voluntary, and consequences of non-provision

name: Disclosure Principle
summary: Personal data shall not be disclosed for any purpose other than the purpose for which it was disclosed at the time of collection without consent.

name: Security Principle
summary: A data controller shall take practical steps to protect personal data from loss, misuse, modification, or unauthorized access.

obligations.obligation: Mandatory Breach Notification to Commissioner (and data subject if significant harm likely)

name: Retention Principle
summary: Personal data shall not be kept longer than is necessary for the fulfilment of that purpose.

name: Data Integrity Principle
summary: A data controller shall take reasonable steps to ensure that personal data is accurate, complete, and kept up to date.

name: Access Principle
summary: A data controller shall allow a data subject to have access to and correct their personal data.

name: Right to Data Portability
summary: Right to have data transmitted to another controller in a structured, machine-readable format.

name: Right to Restriction of Processing
summary: Right to restrict processing in certain circumstances (e.g. while accuracy is being verified).
